title: agentic-stack permissions policy
description: Source-of-truth permission policy. pre_tool_call.py, permissions_render.py, and render_claude_settings.py all read this shape.

always_allowed.description: Human-readable actions that never require approval.

requires_approval.description: Actions that the agent may attempt but a human must greenlight.

never_allowed.description: Hard denies. Each entry may be a plain string (legacy) or a structured block with patterns/targets/keywords for richer matching.

rule.description: Human-readable summary; appears in block messages and rendered markdown.

items.description: Claude-Code-style pattern, e.g. Bash(git push --force*). The pattern is used by both the pre_tool_call hook (via fnmatch) and the rendered settings.json deny list.

targets.description: Literal values (branch names, env names) that match args.branch/args.target/args.env.

keywords.description: Fallback keyword set for the markdown-only enforcement path. 2+ matches in the call description triggers a block.

approved_domains.description: Hosts that HTTP tools may reach. Anything outside this list is implicitly denied.
